mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on future earnings declines and equity valuation multiples expand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is also decelerating, because easing without an inflation rebound is more likely to persist.
failure_condition: Rejected if falling FEDFUNDS does not coincide with above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the signal turns negative in a rolling 3-year window despite CPIAUCSL deceleration.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which compresses expected corporate revenue and earnings. Under that condition, S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if UNRATE breakout episodes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals weakening payroll income and retail spending, which should reduce near-term revenue expectations and push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The effect requires both labor income and consumption to soften together rather than either series alone.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint PAYEMS and RSAFS deceleration does not improve forecast accuracy versus single-series models, or if triggered periods produce non-negative 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in INDPRO reflects stronger real activity and industrial throughput, which supports earnings revisions and cyclical equity sectors. Over a quarter, this should translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns when production growth is broad-based rather than a one-month spike.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive 3-month INDPRO trends fail to precede positive 60d SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year return differential versus declining-INDPRO periods is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing weakness matters when HOUST falls sharply because it reduces construction activity, wealth effects, and rate-sensitive demand. Once housing starts drop below a recent threshold, S&P 500 returns should turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if HOUST breakdown episodes are not followed by below-baseline 20d SPX returns or if the effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising FEDFUNDS rate tightens financial conditions, compresses valuation multiples, and raises recession risk. When policy is above 4.5% and still rising, S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if high-rate episodes yield positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time or if the signal becomes positive during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in PAYEMS and a decline in UNRATE indicates broad labor-market strengthening, which should lift consumer income and support corporate revenues. The effect should be positive over the next week because equity markets react quickly to labor surprise persistence.
failure_condition: Rejected if combined PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines do not outperform either series alone in predicting 5d SPX returns, or if the sign is not positive in triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand recession is present when GDPC1 growth weakens while RSAFS also softens. That combination implies downward earnings revisions and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, especially if labor data do not offset the demand slump.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint GDP and retail slowdown periods fail to produce negative 60d SPX returns, or if adding PAYEMS does not materially improve the hit rate versus GDPC1 and RSAFS alone.
